title: ASSESSMENT_REQUEST
description: Message sent by Claimant to Referee to initiate an assessment. The incident_id is generated by the Claimant at this point.

incident_id.description: Unique identifier for this incident. UUID v4 format, generated by the Claimant.

claimant_id.description: Terminal ID of the Claimant initiating the assessment.

actor_id.description: Terminal ID of the Actor. Optional. Omit for Claimant-only assessments (e.g., Proof of Human Involvement without an opposing Actor).

actor_keeper_endpoint.description: (Optional) Endpoint URL of the Actor's Keeper, if known to the Claimant at filing time. This is the primary delivery route for ACTOR_NOTIFICATION (Message Delivery, RFC-0001 Section 6); a Norm-defined endpoint registry is the fallback. If neither source yields an endpoint, the notification cannot be delivered and the Referee proceeds with a Claimant-only assessment. If provided, the Referee MAY also use this to issue a FEE_STATUS_QUERY before receiving ACTOR_ACKNOWLEDGMENT.

keeper_endpoint.description: Endpoint URL of the Keeper that holds the anchoring records. The Referee will use this to send EVIDENCE_QUERY_REQUEST and VERIFICATION_QUERY.

incident_summary.description: Human-readable summary of the incident. Used by the Referee as the basis for evidence queries.

incident_timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the incident occurred or was detected.

prior_incident_ids.description: (Optional) UUIDs of prior incidents related to this assessment. Used as reference for recurrence or related cases. The Referee MAY use these to retrieve prior CONTRIBUTION_RESULTs from the Keeper.

norm_profile_ids.description: (Optional) Norm Profile IDs to apply for this assessment (reverse-domain notation, e.g., 'rackp.standard.v1'). Multiple IDs are allowed when the Claimant operates under multiple Norms simultaneously. If omitted, the Standard Norm (rackp.standard.v1) is applied.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when this message was sent.
